entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A dryland cereal crop used in the nitrogen fertilizer study.

entities.name: SAFFLOWER
entities.description: A crop grown in the post-rainy season within a double-cropping system.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil in India where the field studies were conducted.

entities.name: UREA
entities.description: A nitrogen fertilizer applied at a rate of 74 kg N/ha.

entities.name: 15N-LABELED FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Fertilizers containing the 15N isotope used to determine the fate of nitrogen.

entities.name: POST-EMERGENCE SPLIT-BAND APPLICATION
entities.description: A urea application method (SB) performed after crop emergence.

entities.name: SURFACE-APPLIED APPLICATION
entities.description: A urea application method (S) where fertilizer is applied to the soil surface.

entities.name: INCORPORATED APPLICATION
entities.description: A urea application method (I) where fertilizer is mixed into the soil.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation during the growing season that affects nitrogen recovery and utilization.

entities.name: DOUBLE-CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: An agricultural system involving the sequential growth of sorghum and safflower.

entities.name: NITROGEN RECOVERY
entities.description: The percentage of applied nitrogen recovered in the soil-plant system after harvest.

entities.name: PLANT UTILIZATION
entities.description: The efficiency with which plants use added urea-nitrogen.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the Vertisol field studies were conducted.
